data.title: After a major whale (a large investor) made a profit of over $10 million on its short position in SOL, it continued to short SOL with 20x leverage.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, the whale (0x35d...acb1) that has the highest profit on its SOL short position continues to increase its SOL short position with a leverage of 20x, and its floating profit has now exceeded $10 million. This whale has also opened a new ETH short position with 25x leverage.

data.title: The 21Shares Solana ETF was launched, and the total net inflow of US Solana spot ETFs reached $55.61 million in a single day.
data.description: On November 19th, Eastern Time, the 21Shares Solana ETF (ticker symbol TSOL) officially listed on the CBOE after receiving SEC approval, bringing the total number of listed Solana spot ETFs in the US to six. According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (November 19th, Eastern Time), Solana spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $55.61 million. TSOL saw no net inflow on its first day of trading, with a trading volume of $400,000 and a total net asset value of $104 million. The ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the Bitwise BSOL ETF, with a net inflow of $35.87 million and a historical total net inflow of $424 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of Solana spot ETFs is $715 million, the Solana net asset ratio is 0.97%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $476 million. The 21Shares Solana ETF supports cash or in-kind creation and redemption, with a management fee of 0.21%. It supports Solana to provide additional returns through staking, with the sponsor receiving a 10% share of the staking returns after deducting the staking service fee.

data.title: Core Foundation Secures Court Injunction Against Maple Finance Over Confidentiality Breach
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the Core Foundation has obtained an injunction from the Grand Court of the Cayman Islands, which found significant issues requiring examination regarding Maple Finance's alleged breach of confidentiality and exclusive cooperation terms during their joint development of lstBTC.

The Core Foundation stated that since the beginning of their partnership in early 2025, they have invested substantial resources in technology development, marketing, and product subsidies. However, Maple Finance has been accused of using confidential information to develop competing products, violating the 24-month exclusive cooperation agreement between the two parties. In response to Maple Finance's announcement of asset impairment in its Bitcoin Yield product, the Core Foundation highlighted the lack of clarity on why Maple Finance is unable to return Bitcoin to lenders, emphasizing that this raises serious concerns about Maple Finance's business practices.

data.title: Bitwise Announces Details of Upcoming XRP ETF
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Bitwise, a cryptocurrency asset management company, has revealed details about its forthcoming Bitwise XRP ETF. The ETF will have a management fee of 0.34%, which will be waived until the assets under management reach $500 million. It will be listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol XRP. This ETF marks the 49th cryptocurrency investment product launched by Bitwise in the United States and Europe.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin warns Wall Street institutions of two major threats to Ethereum.
data.description: At Devconnect, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin warned that Ethereum faces two risks if BlackRock and other large institutions continue to increase their Ethereum holdings: (1) builders with decentralized ideas could be squeezed out, weakening the community; and (2) institutionally optimized base layer choices (e.g., block times of around 150 ms) could prevent ordinary users from running nodes, thus driving geographical/network centralization. Currently, nine Wall Street firms offering Ethereum ETFs hold over $18 billion in Ether, with corporate treasuries holding an additional $18 billion. Buterin emphasized that Ethereum should focus on a “global, permissionless, and censorship-resistant protocol” to maintain its unique value.

data.title: Bitget Wallet launches the "Steady Profits in US Stocks - Earn Money While You Sleep" campaign with a total prize pool of $550,000.
data.description: Bitget Wallet will launch the "US Stocks Steady Profits, Passive Income Season" campaign from November 19th to December 26th, 2025. Users can participate and earn rewards through two methods: holding tokenized US stock spot positions and trading US stock futures. In the "Hold and Win US Stocks" campaign, users who purchase and hold any tokenized US stock spot positions will receive weekly rewards with an annualized return of up to 50%. Rewards will be distributed in the form of popular stock tokens such as TSLA, NVDA, AAPL, and GOOGL, with a total prize pool of $500,000. The value of holdings is calculated in real time, and the previous week's earnings will be settled and distributed every Sunday at 23:59:59 (the first week of the campaign will be settled on Monday). The "Trade US Stocks, Earn US Stocks" campaign will run from November 19th to 28th, 2025, with a total prize pool of $50,000 and 4,000 winners. The top ten users on the leaderboard can receive up to $4,000 worth of stock tokens, while users ranked 101st to 2,000th will each receive a reward equivalent to $20. The event tracks all trading volume from opening to closing positions.

data.title: Brazilian Crypto Users Warned of WhatsApp-Based Hacking Threat
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Brazilian cryptocurrency holders are being cautioned about a sophisticated hacking campaign that employs a hijacking worm and banking trojan disseminated through WhatsApp messages. Trustwave’s cybersecurity research team, SpiderLabs, has identified the banking trojan, named 'Eternidade Stealer,' which is being spread via social engineering tactics on WhatsApp. These tactics include fake government programs, delivery notifications, messages from friends, and fraudulent investment groups. SpiderLabs researchers Nathaniel Morales, John Basmayor, and Nikita Kazymirskyi noted that WhatsApp remains one of the most exploited communication channels in Brazil's cybercrime ecosystem
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/32621389116129?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: Delayed U.S. September Jobs Data to Be Released Amid Economic Uncertainty
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the release of the U.S. September non-farm payroll data, initially scheduled for October 3, was postponed due to the government shutdown and is set to be announced tonight. The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics had completed data collection and analysis before the shutdown, ensuring minimal impact on data quality. During the October Federal Reserve meeting, interest rates were cut despite insufficient data, with no commitment to future rate cuts. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell indicated that the October rate cut was a "risk management" decision and suggested significant internal disagreements within the Fed.

As the first major economic data release following the government shutdown, the September jobs report could serve as a crucial catalyst for future market trends if it provides new insights. Key macroeconomic data to be released at 9:30 PM UTC+8 includes the U.S. September unemployment rate, seasonally adjusted non-farm employment figures, and initial jobless claims for the week ending November 15.

data.title: The crypto market consolidated, with Layer 2 stocks rising 0.52% and BTC rebounding above $92,000.
data.description: On November 20th, according to SoSoValue data, the crypto market as a whole fluctuated and consolidated. The Layer 2 sector remained relatively strong, rising 0.52% in the last 24 hours, with Starknet (STRK) up 17.49% and zkSync (ZK) up 15.23%. Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC) rose 0.09%, rebounding above $92,000, while Ethereum (ETH) fell 1.93%, fluctuating narrowly around $3,000. Additionally, the NFT sector rose 0.33% in the last 24 hours, with Zora (ZORA) up 4.82%; the AI ​​sector rose 0.18%, with Fetch.ai (FET) up 9.45%. In other sectors, the DeFi sector fell 1.18%, but Hyperliquid (HYPE) rose 1.13%; the Layer 1 sector fell 1.30%, while Zcash (ZEC) bucked the trend, rising 12.07%; the Meme sector fell 2.03%, with Pump.fun (PUMP) surging 4.41% intraday; the CeFi sector fell 2.58%, with Mantle (MNT) rising 1.18%; and the PayFi sector fell 3.54%, with Dash (DASH) rising 5.75%. The crypto sector index, reflecting historical sector performance, shows that ssiDeFi, ssiGameFi, and ssiSocialFi fell 3.90%, 3.17%, and 3.11%, respectively.

data.title: Brazilian Crypto Users Warned of WhatsApp-Based Hacking Threat
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Brazilian cryptocurrency holders are being cautioned about a sophisticated hacking campaign that employs a hijacking worm and banking trojan disseminated through WhatsApp messages. Trustwave’s cybersecurity research team, SpiderLabs, has identified the banking trojan, named 'Eternidade Stealer,' which is being spread via social engineering tactics on WhatsApp. These tactics include fake government programs, delivery notifications, messages from friends, and fraudulent investment groups.

SpiderLabs researchers Nathaniel Morales, John Basmayor, and Nikita Kazymirskyi noted that WhatsApp remains one of the most exploited communication channels in Brazil's cybercrime ecosystem. Over the past two years, cybercriminals have refined their strategies, leveraging the platform's widespread popularity to distribute banker trojans and information-stealing malware. The process begins when a user clicks on a worm link in WhatsApp, triggering a chain reaction that infects the device with both the worm and banking trojan.

The worm hijacks the victim's account and accesses their contact list, employing 'smart filtering' to bypass business contacts and groups, thereby targeting individual contacts more efficiently. Concurrently, the banking trojan automatically downloads onto the victim's device, deploying the Eternidade Stealer in the background. This malware scans for financial data and logins to various Brazilian banks, fintech companies, and crypto exchanges and wallets.

The malware employs a clever method to avoid detection or shutdown. Instead of using a fixed server address, it utilizes a pre-set Gmail account to receive new commands via email, allowing hackers to update commands by sending new emails. The report highlights that the malware uses hardcoded credentials to log into its email account, from which it retrieves its command and control (C2) server. This approach helps maintain persistence and evade network-level detections or takedowns. If the malware cannot connect to the email account, it resorts to a hardcoded fallback C2 address.

Users of apps like WhatsApp are advised to exercise caution with any links sent to them, even from trusted contacts. A prudent approach is to verify the link's legitimacy by messaging the sender on a different app. Additionally, users should be wary of links sent unexpectedly with limited context. Keeping software updated can protect against bugs targeting older versions, while antivirus software may help flag potential issues. In the event of a hack, it is crucial to immediately freeze all access points to banking and crypto services to prevent further damage. Tracking funds can assist exchanges, researchers, or authorities in tracing the assets' movement, potentially aiding in freezing hacker wallets.
